question_title: Will the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission post a new advanced-reactor licensing milestone before April 30, 2026?
summary: The NRC has been posting advanced reactor licensing milestones at an unprecedented pace in early 2026: TRISO-X fuel fabrication license (Feb 13), TerraPower Natrium construction permit (March 4), and Oklo/Atomic Alchemy materials license (March 17). The resolution window of March 15 - April 30 spans 46 days during a period of historically high NRC activity, with multiple applications in the pipeline (Kairos Power, Oklo Aurora, X-energy reactor), executive orders pushing accelerated timelines, and the ADVANCE Act compressing licensing deadlines. The implied milestone rate is roughly one every 12 days. The probability of zero qualifying milestones appearing on the NRC advanced reactors pages over 46 days is very low.

factors.description: Extremely high pace of NRC advanced reactor activity in early 2026 - three major milestones in 5 weeks (TerraPower permit, TRISO-X license, Oklo materials license)
Multiple advanced reactor applications actively in NRC pipeline (Kairos Power Hermes, Oklo Aurora, X-energy Xe-100, etc.) - any acceptance/milestone could trigger YES
46-day resolution window (March 15 - April 30) is substantial, giving many opportunities for milestones
Executive orders and ADVANCE Act pushing NRC to accelerate licensing timelines, increasing likelihood of near-term milestones
NRC advanced reactors pages are actively maintained and regularly updated with new items
Possible that all major milestones cluster in early March and a lull follows, though unlikely given pipeline depth

reasoning:
My forecast is **0.95** (95% probability of YES).

**Key reasoning:**
- The NRC has been extraordinarily active on advanced reactor licensing in early 2026, with three major milestones in just 5 weeks
- The 46-day resolution window (March 15 - April 30) provides ample opportunity for at least one qualifying milestone to appear on the NRC's advanced reactors pages
- Multiple applications are actively in the NRC pipeline, and executive pressure is pushing faster timelines
- The only NO scenario requires a complete 46-day lull in an area experiencing unprecedented activity — highly unlikely given the pipeline depth
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the NRC’s advanced reactors pages at https://www.nrc.gov/reactors/new-reactors/advanced show, or link to, a new item dated March 15, 2026 through April 30, 2026 inclusive that reports acceptance of an application, issuance of a permit or license, or another explicit licensing milestone for an advanced reactor or advanced-fuel facility before April 30, 2026. Otherwise, it resolves to no.
fine_print: General policy statements, hearings, and comment requests do not count unless they clearly report an application acceptance, permit issuance, license issuance, or similarly explicit licensing milestone. Candidate Sources: NRC news releases at https://www.nrc.gov/reading-rm/doc-collections/news/ ; specific NRC licensing pages linked from the advanced reactors section.
